what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the D1 foundational how-to/affordance stratum. It verifies that a ui:// address's authored howto plumbs from the corpus/registry into the live system, that _r2_howto_at gathers it as a pinned, re-stamped, flood-proof R2 item, that address_help joins the what-this-is, how-to-change, and how-to-use legs, and that missing legs degrade cleanly while malformed addresses fail loud.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 and builds a fresh Suite with a temp FsStore and NodeRegistry for each test section.
Uses a hardcoded SEEDED address ui://toolbar/run and dynamically discovers an UNAUTHORED address by scanning ui_info().
Tests _registry_howto_for for authored howto, honest None absence, and loud failure on malformed addresses.
Tests _r2_howto_at returns a single kind='howto' item that is pinned=True, has no '_raw' key, ts==now, and is empty for unauthored addresses.
Proves pin-persistence by showing the howto's _r2_score is identical 30 days later because ts is re-stamped to now each gather.
Proves flood-proofing by monkey-patching a giant howto onto UI_REGISTRY and asserting truncation plus coexistence with an operator comment within R2_BUDGET.
Tests address_help returns a bundle with address, what_this_is, how_to_change, how_to_use, and legs_present.
Tests degradation: unauthored address yields how_to_use=None with other legs present; malformed address raises instead of returning a partial bundle.
Uses a global PASS counter and a check() helper that exits immediately on failure.
standouts: Dynamic fixture selection for UNAUTHORED to avoid silent breakage when corpus howto coverage changes.
Pin-persistence proven through score equality over 30 days using the existing scorer, not a new mechanism.
Flood-proof test monkey-patches UI_REGISTRY row extras to validate truncation bound without a separate code path.
address_help explicitly joins three legs and degrades cleanly when one is absent.
conventions: Follows the sibling acceptance-test pattern of fresh_suite per sub-test and COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1.
Uses real corpus/registry rather than mocks, consistent with acceptance-by-use.
Names and tests internal underscore-prefixed methods directly.
Breaks typical immutability convention by mutating UI_REGISTRY in the flood-proof test, but documents it as intentional data injection.
concerns: check() calls SystemExit on the first failure, so no aggregated failure report is possible.
UNAUTHORED discovery runs address_help over every ui address at import time, which can be slow and makes module import fail if no unauthored address exists.
Flood-proof test couples tightly to UI_REGISTRY row structure (tuple index 5 extras).
Hardcoded SEEDED address could break if corpus seed changes.
Temp directories created by fresh_suite are not explicitly cleaned up.
Heavy reliance on internal methods makes the test brittle to refactors of the R2 pipeline.
notes: An acceptance test that exercises the D1 how-to affordance layer end-to-end, emphasizing pin-persistence, flood-proofing, and clean degradation.
questions: What is the exact schema of UI_REGISTRY rows and the row[5] extras whitelist?
How does _r2_dedup distinguish kind='howto' from 'event', and why must the howto item omit '_raw'?
What are the numeric values of R2_HOWTO_MAX and R2_BUDGET?
What does address_help return when how_to_change has no blast_radius but has scope?
How does _describe_ui_address produce labels, and what does '(unregistered)' indicate?
What is the intended E1 mode-parameterizable extension mentioned but not implemented?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it proves data plumbing, resolver behavior, composed address_help, and clean degradation as described in the module docstring.
apparent_themes: Pin-persistence and bounded, non-starving help content
Reuse-only architecture with no parallel store
Dynamic fixture selection to avoid silent false positives
intention_signals: The file is trying to validate that foundational UI address help is wired through the existing R2 gather/score/cap pipeline, remains stable over time, stays within budget, and degrades gracefully when content is missing.
novelty: It treats help content as a pinned, non-decaying R2 item alongside events and annotations, and proves persistence by score equality rather than by adding a special-case decay rule.
contribution: A concrete, executable contract for D1 how-to affordance behavior, covering corpus-to-registry plumbing, pinned flood-proof gathering, three-leg address_help composition, and degradation semantics.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the D1 how-to/affordance stratum that verifies corpus-to-registry howto plumbing, _r2_howto_at pinned flood-proof gathering, address_help three-leg composition, and clean degradation.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by real use that foundational UI address help resolves, persists, stays bounded, and degrades cleanly.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that prints PASS/FAIL, increments a global counter, and exits immediately on failure.
symbols.description: The check(label, cond) function is the local test harness: it increments PASS when cond is true and prints a PASS line; otherwise it prints FAIL and raises SystemExit(1), halting the suite on the first failure.

symbols.does: Builds a new Suite with a temp FsStore and a NodeRegistry that discovers the nodes directory.
symbols.description: fresh_suite() constructs a clean runtime environment for each test section by creating a NodeRegistry, discovering nodes under ROOT/nodes, and instantiating a Suite backed by a fresh temporary FsStore.
